item.title: Clerical tone/feedback
item.content:encoded:
First off, I'd like to say this is the best SCP ive seen in some 200. Like all things on this green earth however, its flawed.

Most notably the part where the article mentioned it wanting to come to earth. It's a break of clerical tone, and repeated in the note somewhere lower down.
Another issue with this is how the foundation knows for sure that it wants on earth. Nitpicking aside, outstanding work.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Currently, you have not sourced the image used in this article, and as such may be a violation of the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0). Because you have received more than 48 hours to cite the image, without the source being given, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4571's facial appearance is reminiscent of containing both canine and feline features.

This sentence is really awkward and it's hard to tell exactly what you mean. Might I suggest changing it to something like

SCP-4571 instances contain facial features reminiscent of both canines and felines

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That image is badly photoshopped into the background.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh I never knew kelvin didn't use degrees. Thanks for the info!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah I second Rigen's comment, Kelvin doesn't use degrees. Other than that, I liked reading through it. Might be just a bit confusing to read, and I think it might've been interesting to have some formatting and styling because it's so long. Either way, it's an interesting piece!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really enjoy this SCP. It's really unique compared to what I usually read. Great work :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
██° degrees Kelvin.

You used both degree symbol and the word degree for the one temperature scale that doesn't use degree.
